summary: This notebook demonstrates a complete time-series gesture recognition pipeline for multi-sensor data (IMU, TOF, thermal). It highlights a key preprocessing step: using quaternion orientation data to project and subtract the gravity vector from raw accelerometer readings, isolating true linear acceleration for cleaner motion signals. The author constructs a two-branch neural network that processes IMU and TOF/thermal features separately before fusing them into a hybrid BiLSTM+GRU+Attention architecture. Training leverages MixUp augmentation, class weighting, and cosine decay learning rate scheduling to handle imbalance and improve generalization. Readers should learn how quaternion-based gravity removal enhances gesture discrimination and how a CNN-RNN-Attention hybrid effectively captures both local spatial patterns and long-range temporal dependencies in sensor time series.
competition_flow: Loads raw sensor CSVs, applies quaternion-based gravity removal and kinematic feature engineering, aggregates TOF pixel data, scales and pads sequences, trains a two-branch CNN-BiLSTM-GRU-Attention model with MixUp and class weights, and exports the model for inference.
data_reading: pd.read_csv for train.csv and train_demographics.csv, merged on subject column.
data_processing: ffill/bfill/fillna(0) for missing values, StandardScaler fitted on concatenated training steps, sequence padding to 95th percentile length, MixUp augmentation (alpha=0.4), class weight balancing, cosine decay LR scheduling, early stopping.
features_engineering: Quaternion-based gravity removal yielding linear_acc_x/y/z, linear_acc_mag, linear_acc_mag_jerk.
TOF pixel aggregation: mean, std, min, max per sensor.
Cross-sensor contrast features: range and std across sensors for TOF and thermal data.
models: Two-branch architecture: residual SE-CNN blocks (IMU), lightweight CNNs (TOF/Thermal), Bidirectional LSTM, Bidirectional GRU, GaussianNoise, Dense, Attention.
insights: Quaternion-based gravity removal isolates true linear acceleration, significantly improving gesture discrimination.
MixUp augmentation and class weighting effectively regularize the model and handle class imbalance.
A hybrid CNN-RNN-Attention architecture successfully captures both local spatial patterns and long-range temporal dependencies in multi-sensor time series.
what_did_not_work: Further reducing the initial learning rate to 2e-4 worsened the validation score.
Adjusting dropout rates provided no improvement over the baseline configuration.
Replacing the simpler CNN in the TOF/Thermal branch with residual SE blocks was detrimental to performance.
